guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Dermatology / Antibiotic — boils, furuncles and cutaneous abscess
Therapeutic Guidelines: Antibiotic — cellulitis (mild, oral flucloxacillin)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who performed incision and drainage (I&D) of a pointing abscess under local anaesthetic — the AI Doctor CANNOT drain an abscess or give an injectable anaesthetic, and is scored on recognising a mature fluctuant abscess with surrounding cellulitis + systemic features and arranging urgent same-day in-person drainage plus oral antibiotics.

Over/under-triage calibration: a lay 'boil' label that is actually a large pointing abscess with spreading cellulitis and low-grade systemic upset — the trap is under-triaging to self-care/warm-compresses-only (misses that a mature abscess needs drainage and cellulitis needs antibiotics) OR over-triaging a systemically-stable patient to 000/ED.
Error of commission tested: advising the patient to squeeze/pop the lesion themselves, or prescribing oral antibiotics alone with no arrangement for drainage.

Important-not-to-miss screen: posterior-neck location with neck stiffness must be actively screened for deep neck-space infection / airway involvement (screened negative here — no dysphagia, drooling, trismus or voice change).
Age and sex not stated in source — inferred; flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-009.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
